meta.name: Luke Quarderer
meta.title: Engineering Portfolio
meta.intro: Selected projects from my engineering degree — designed, built, tested, and documented. Each entry lists the skills applied, the tools used, and the artefacts produced as evidence of the work.
meta.seoDescription: Luke Quarderer's engineering portfolio — Aerospace Engineering projects from the University of Michigan, documented with skills, tools, and outcomes.
meta.aboutBio:
I'm a second-year Aerospace Engineering student in the College of Engineering at the University of Michigan, with a particular interest in applying aerospace technologies within the defense sector. I'm building a strong technical foundation through coursework, hands-on projects, and collaborative experiences, and I'm looking for opportunities where engineering innovation meets real-world defense challenges.

As a U.S. expat, I currently live in Canberra, Australia. Six years spent overseas as a diplomat's dependent in London and Canberra gave me a close-up view of the intersection between government, military, and diplomacy.
This past year, I interned with Leidos Australia, supporting data management and automation solutions used by more than 45 members of the Sea Archer program. That experience reinforced how much I value learning through industry work alongside my coursework.

Outside of academics, I love to travel — this year I backpacked through Colombia and Vanuatu, bringing my country count to 31, with the Dominican Republic next on the list.
